content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals (from ICM/RND comparative research) can act as a dynamic self-supervision signal that prunes and prioritizes unlabeled episodic memory storage in self-supervised world model agents, solving the common problem of memory bloat in long-horizon task settings. This creates a curious self-supervised agent that only stores surprising, learnable experiences instead of accumulating every encountered observation.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
